subject: Project Management
title: Wikipedia
description: A short lesson to help you apply Wikipedia.

hook.text: In 2001, Jimmy Wales launched an encyclopedia that anyone could edit. Experts called it insane.

buildup.text: Volunteers wrote articles for free. No editors, no publishers, no gatekeepers.

discovery.text: Wikipedia grew to 6 million English articles — more than any encyclopedia in history.

twist.text: Studies found Wikipedia's accuracy rivaled Encyclopaedia Britannica. The crowd was surprisingly wise.

climax.text: Wikipedia became the world's reference — a project that never ends, run by volunteers.

punchline.text: The encyclopedia nobody controls is the one everybody uses.

quiz.question: What surprised researchers about Wikipedia's accuracy?
options: It rivaled traditional encyclopedias like Britannica
